text.title: PM Certification Alignment 2026 Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding PM Certification Alignment 2026. Each pairs a binary check and a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each methodology MUST be tagged with primary ECO 2026 domain ∈ {People, Process, Business Environment} and theme.
rule.rationale: Untagged methodologies cannot inform candidate study plans; explicit tagging makes coverage measurable.

rule.statement: Coverage analysis MUST compare methodology count per domain against ECO weight percentages (People 33%, Process 41%, Business Environment 26%).
rule.rationale: Coverage gaps correlate with exam-section underperformance; weight-vs-count comparison surfaces them.

rule.statement: Theme tags MUST come from the five canonical 2026 exam themes; ad-hoc themes forbidden.
rule.rationale: Ad-hoc themes fragment the rubric; five-theme constraint preserves comparability across cohorts.

rule.statement: Each coverage gap MUST trigger an action item with owner + due date (e.g. author missing methodology by Q3-2026).
rule.rationale: Gaps without actions persist for years; the rule forces backlog placement.

rule.statement: Candidate study plan MUST cite the rubric output; ad-hoc study plans forbidden.
rule.rationale: Ad-hoc study plans rely on instructor intuition; evidence-based plans tune time per ECO weight.

rule.statement: If preconditions for PM Certification Alignment 2026 fail (see Skip-If list in AGENTS.md), the operator MUST skip this methodology and document the fallback reason instead of fabricating an output.
rule.rationale: Applying PM Certification Alignment 2026 outside its envelope produces false-precision artefacts. Explicit skip preserves epistemic discipline.
